title: Spider-Woman
content:
{{Otheruses|Spider-Woman}} {{Stub}} {{Characters |name = Spider-Woman |image = Julia Carpenter.jpg |real_name = Julia Cornwall (birth name)
Julia Carpenter[[:File:Madame Web - Resolutions - In Cinemas February 16 - -Shorts|Madame Web - Resolutions | In Cinemas February 16 | #Shorts]] |alias = {{Alias |codenames = Spider-Woman}} |species = [[Humans|Human]] |gender = Female |affiliation = [[Boo Crew]] |citizenship = {{Citizenship|USA}} |status = Alive |movie = ''[[Madame Web (film)|Madame Web]]''
''[[Spider-Woman (film)|Spider-Woman]]'' (unreleased)[https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-news/donald-glover-to-star-in-spider-man-movie-hypno-hustler-1235283667/ Donald Glover to Star in, Produce Spider-Man Movie Based on Villain Hypno-Hustler (Exclusive)] |portrayed_by = [[Sydney Sweeney]]}} {{Quote|You have no idea what [[Boo Crew|those girls]] become.|[[Ezekiel Sims]] to [[Cassie Webb]]|Madame Web (film)}} '''Julia Carpenter''', born '''Julia Cornwall''', is a young woman that gained [[Spiders|spider]]-like abilities, becoming known as '''Spider-Woman'''. In her youth, she, alongside a [[Boo Crew|group of other women]] was being chased by [[Ezekiel Sims]], who wanted them dead, which result in them being protected by Cassandra Webb. In the end, they were saved and Julia could complete her destiny of becoming a vigilante heroine. ==Biography== === Early Life === ==== Dysfunctional Family==== Growing up, Julia was bullied at school, taking taekwondo. Sometime prior to 2003, her parents divorced with her mother suffering a mental episode that had her committed and Julia was sent to live with her father and his new family. Despite her putting in the effort to try and connect, her new family remained distant.''[[Madame Web (film)|Madame Web]]'' ====Step-Mother's Accident==== In 2003, Julia's stepmother suffered an accident and was rushed to the hospital by the paramedics of the [[New York City Fire Department]]. Though her stepmother was stabilized, Julia was asked by her father not to join them in seeing her. ===[[Pursuit of the Boo Crew|Hunted by Ezekiel Sims]]=== ====[[Massacre at Grand Central Terminal]]==== Feeling that her family didn't care about her, Julia decided to run away from home, boarding a train at Grand Central Station. Along with [[Araña|Anya Corazon]] and [[Spider-Girl|Mattie Franklin]], she was ushered off the train by [[Madame Web|Cassandra Webb]] to protect her from [[Ezekiel Sims]]. When the four stole a taxi as a getaway car, Cassie heard a news broadcast labelling her as having kidnapped the three. ==== Left in the Forest==== Cassie took the girls to a forest, somewhere away from any cameras, where the three realized that they had all crossed paths with Cassie in some way. Following Cassie heading back to [[New York City]] for answers, Anya and Mattie thought to go to a [[4 Star Diner|nearby diner]]. Though Julia protested, she was peer-pressured into tagging along, Anya finding Julia to be cute when angry. ====[[Attack on 4 Star Diner]]==== ''To be added'' ====Helped by Cassie Webb==== ''To be added'' ====[[Ambush on United States Postal Service]]==== ''To be added'' ====[[Battle at Pepsi-Cola Bottling Plant]]==== ''To be added'' ===[[Boo Crew]]=== ====Living with the Boo Crew==== ''To be added'' ====Far Future==== ''To be added'' ==Personality== ''To be added'' == Physical Appearance == * A light-colored, possibly of white or cream, but enough jacket or blazer with a structured color and lapels. * A white button-up shirt underneath the jacket, featuring a black blanket with black buttons in the white color, getting into tiny fact, a dark grey, pleated miniskirt a black over one shoulder glass with a thin dark frame. She is later seen wearing a light grey, possibly ribbed, cropped top that exposes her midriff and bellybutton. * hoody, over the crop top, she’s wearing a matching light grey zip up Hoodie worn open. * accessories she’s also wearing glasses in necklace. * bottoms, dark coloured pants, likely jeans, or similar visible below the crop top. == Powers and Abilities == == Potential Powers == *'''Spider Physiology''': In the future, Julia Carpenter would receive her [[Spiders|spider]] powers, as seen by [[Ezekiel Sims]] and [[Madame Web]]'s [[Web of Life|visions]]. **'''Superhuman Agility''': ''To be added'' **'''Psionic Webbing''': Julia could produce a form of mental webbing as a form of offense and defense. == Abilities == *'''Martial Artist''': Julia Cornwall mentioned she trained in {{WPS|Taekwondo}} when younger. ==Equipment== *'''[[Spider-Woman Suit]]''': A specialized black and white suit would be created and used by Julia Cornwall to operate as the crime-fighting heroine known as Spider-Woman. ==Relationships== {{Col-begin}}{{Col-2}} ===Family=== *[[Mr. Cornwall]] - Father *Mother *Step-Mother *Half-Brother ===Allies=== *[[New York City Fire Department]] **[[Madame Web|Cassie Webb/Madame Web]] - Friend, Mentor and Savior **[[Ben Parker]] *[[Boo Crew]] - Friends **[[Araña|Anya Corazón/Araña]] **[[Spider-Girl|Mattie Franklin/Spider-Girl]] *[[Mary Parker]] {{Col-2}} ===Enemies=== *[[Ezekiel Sims]] † - Attempted Killer {{Col-end}} ==Trivia== *In the comics, Julia Carpenter is a woman who became a test subject injected with [[Spiders|spider]] venom and exotic plant extracts, turning into the Spider-Woman. She also took on the moniker, '''[[Madame Web]]''', after [[Cassandra Webb]]'s death. **The first Spider-Woman was '''Jessica Drew''', who was once an ill child kept alive as she was given a serum made from spider DNA. **'''[[Mattie Franklin]]''' also takes the mantle of Spider-Woman. **'''Charlotte Witter''' also takes the mantle of Spider-Woman. ==Behind the Scenes== *[[Rachel Klatsky]] and [[Michaela McAllister]] were stunt doubles for [[Sydney Sweeney]] in the role of Spider-Woman. ==References== {{Gallery|Spider-Woman/Gallery}} {{Wq|Spider-Woman}} {{Reflist}} ==External Links== *{{Marvel}} *{{Marvel|Julia Cornwall}} *{{Marvel|Julia Carpenter}} *{{WP}} *{{WP|Julia Carpenter}} [[Category:Characters]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Females]] [[Category:Heroes]] [[Category:Madame Web (film) Characters]] [[Category:Spider-Woman (film) Characters]] [[es:Julia Carpenter]]
